Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC Case 2: RA -> VF -> AE -> AD -> SC -> AC Case 3: RA -> VF -> AE -> RMI -> RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC Key Observations RA is always first VF follows RA AE follows VF After AE theres a loop optional path RMI can occur 0 or more times After RMI s VF occurs again

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: core flow is RA -> VF -> AE -> loop RMI -> VF -> AD -> SC -> AC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: * ( RMI VF ) is a loop A is executed then either exit or execute B and then A again

Name: Validation - NE
Text: If A = RMI B = VF execute RMI then either exit trace RMI or execute VF then RMI again trace RMI VF RMI This would give alternating RMI and VF Not matching

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: standard semantics in PM4Py Operator LOOP first child loop body second child loop exit execute loop body then either execute loop exit or repeat loop body So * (RMI VF) traces RMI VF RMI RMI VF RMI RMI RMI VF This matches perfectly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2 has no RMI and no VF after AE so the loop is optional Use XOR X( tau * ( RMI VF ) )

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Verify against log Case 1 AE RMI VF AD matches loop once Case 2 AE AD matches tau Case 3 AE RMI RMI VF AD matches loop twice Perfect fit

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Use standard process tree representation in PM4Py context * ( RMI VF ) for repeat RMI then VF Nested binary -> X tau * RMI VF
